creator: albert.shih@obspm.fr
text:
Hi every one.

It's not really a but report but a feature request.

Can we add a option inside 

/etc/periodic/security/100.chksetuid

so we can avoid either some filesystem (like zfs) or better avoid some partition according to some regexp ?

If I ask it's because with the size of the current disk and the fact you can buy some very large storage enclosure, we got very often freebsd server with more than 300-400To of data with lot of lot of files.

So the chksetuid take lots of time to finish (days) so I had to disable it. But I find sometime useful for /, /usr (and so on) but not (/data_like).

Same thing about 110.neggrperm

If "you" like I can propose something...but I'm not a actually dev.

Regards.
